0.title: Authorizes East Providence to issue licenses to any business seeking to engage in the business of operating a hotel, motel, inn, boardinghouse, or catering and that business first obtains approval from chief of police, building inspector and the mayor.

1.last_action: Introduced, referred to Senate Housing and Municipal Government
1.title: Amends the state building code and would require that the state adopt the 2024 International Residential Code and prohibit the state building committee from adopting amendments for one to four residential dwelling units until 2030.

2.last_action: Committee recommended measure be held for further study
2.title: Permits the construction of residential buildings that are up to four (4) stories and sixteen (16) dwelling units with a single staircase under certain conditions.

3.title: Amends the state building code and would require that the state adopt the 2024 International Residential Code and prohibit the state building committee from adopting amendments for one to four residential dwelling units until 2030.

4.title: Excludes from the definition of an "employee" any individual employed in catering, commissary, or food establishment, for purposes of the provisions requiring payment of wages at one and one-half normal rate of pay for work on holidays and Sundays.

5.title: Provides a tax credit for food donations by qualified taxpayers to nonprofit organizations up to five thousand dollars ($5,000) per year.

6.title: Provides a tax credit for food donations by qualified taxpayers to nonprofit organizations up to five thousand dollars ($5,000) per year.

7.last_action: Introduced, referred to House Municipal Government & Housing
7.title: Distinguishes residential and commercial short-term rental properties for tax purposes.

8.last_action: Introduced, referred to House Municipal Government & Housing
8.title: ROOM) ACT (Expands access to inherently affordable housing by re-legalizing co-living/single-room occupancy (SRO) and shared dwelling models of many types.

9.last_action: Introduced, referred to House Health & Human Services
9.title: Removes obsolete language and clarifies asbestos abatement and radon control requirements.

10.last_action: Committee recommended measure be held for further study
10.title: Removes obsolete language and clarifies asbestos abatement and radon control requirements.

11.title: Establishes sales and use tax exemptions for commercial forest product operations. Also creates a new motor vehicle registration for "forestry vehicles".

12.title: Exempts from the sales tax scalp hair prosthesis or wig that are necessary due to hair loss from a medical condition.

13.title: Exempts from the sales tax any prescriptive scalp hair prosthesis or wig that are necessary due to hair loss to treat a medical condition.

14.title: Exempts from the sales tax scalp hair prosthesis or wigs that are necessary due to hair loss from a medical condition.

15.last_action: Committee recommended measure be held for further study
15.title: Establishes property, tangible, sales and use tax exemptions as incentives for the location of qualified data centers in Rhode Island.

16.title: Establishes property, tangible, sales and use tax exemptions as incentives for the location of qualified data centers in Rhode Island.

17.last_action: Committee recommended measure be held for further study
17.title: Mandates the workweek be reduced to 32 hrs. Rate of pay for a 32 hr workweek remains the same as the rate of pay for 40 hrs. Work in excess of 32 hrs in any one workweek qualify for overtime pay. Does not apply to employers with less than 500 employees.
